texto: If you spent time thinking about the future, you wouldn't be a true adventurer. An adventure is something that happens from one moment to the next; in which there is no yesterday and no tomorrow. Everything else is just petty bourgeois.

texto: The staying awake was a great self-sacrificaing gesture of friendship, and wonderfully in keeping with our current mood of intense friendship and religious fervour. We were all in a state of shock. We engaged in a long Dostojevskyan conversations and drank one black coffee after another. It was sort of night typical of youth, the sort you only can look back on with shame and embarassment once you've grown up. But God knows, I must have grown up already by then, because I don't feel the slightest embarassment when I think back to it, just a terrible nostalgia.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Antal Szerb sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “The staying awake was a great self-sacrificaing gesture of friendship, and wonderfully in keeping…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, coffee, conversations, útil em momentos de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: Tell me,' he asked, with some embarassment, as we strolled along: 'you're a bloody German, aren't you?''Oh, no. I'm Hungarian.''Hungarian?''Hungarian.''What's that? Is that a country? Or you are just having me on?'Not at all. On my word of honour, it is a country.''And where do you Hungarians live?''In Hungary. Between Austria, Romania, Czechoslovakia and Yugoslavia'.'Come off it. Those places were made up by Shakespeare.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Antal Szerb que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Tell me,' he asked, with some embarassment, as we strolled along: 'you're a bloody German, aren't…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, humour, hungarian, com foco em reflexao.

texto: The French by their nature had a permanent hunger for sensation. This was even more true of the eighteenth century, of which that considerable expert Victor du Bled remarked that no other age was ever so bored.
